behavior_summary: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit is a guarded hit handler for an NPC (lightning-zapper behavior). When the process receives a hit with a nonzero attacker/item parameter, it checks a guard flag (alreadyHitLightningZapper) and whether the incoming item overlaps this NPC. If conditions pass, it sets the guard to prevent re-triggering, spawns short-lived helper timers, creates and positions a temporary visual item, plays a lightning SFX, animates that temporary item across several frames with timed suspends, destroys the visual item, calls receiveHitWrapper to apply the actual hit/damage to the NPC (using the NPC's facing direction), then clears the guard flag so future hits may trigger again.

The sequence uses spawned helper processes and suspend calls to pace animation and timing; the visual item is created (Item::create), placed (Item::pop), advanced through frames (Item::setFrame), and cleaned up (Item::destroy). The guard flag prevents repeated rapid retriggers.
notable_control_flow: Early return if the incoming param (attacker/item handle) is 0.
Guard check: only proceed if global flag alreadyHitLightningZapper == 0.
Overlap test: Item::overlaps(param1, this) must return true to proceed.

If triggered: set alreadyHitLightningZapper = 1, run the visual/sfx/animation sequence with spawn + suspend pacing, apply damage via METHOD::receiveHitWrapper, then reset alreadyHitLightningZapper = 0.
The sequence spawns multiple helper processes (FREE::func1C19, FREE::func29D8) and calls process LightningBolt; these are used as timers/delays (inferred).
open_questions: Exact numeric effects passed to METHOD::receiveHitWrapper (damage, knockback) are inferred from constants but the gameplay balance intent is not fully confirmed.
The internal behavior of the spawned helpers (FREE::func1C19, FREE::func29D8) is not present here — do they only provide timing jitter or additional visual variation?
Whether the LightningBolt process is purely visual or also contributes to damage/effects is inferred but not certain.
Precise timing / frame counts for the temporary item's animation loop are inferred from loop-like structure but exact durations are unknown.

main_function.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Respond to an incoming hit by running a guarded lightning visual/sfx sequence and then applying the hit to the NPC, preventing immediate retriggering via a guard flag.
entry_conditions: Process invoked as U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit(uword, word) when this NPC receives a hit.
Global flag alreadyHitLightningZapper must be 0 (not recently triggered).
Item::overlaps(param1, this) must return true (incoming item overlaps this NPC).

function_map.name: U8P_LIGTZAP::gotHit
function_map.role: guarded hit handler that runs an animation/sfx sequence and applies damage

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the incoming item/attacker (param1) overlaps this NPC (collision test gating the sequence).
Generate a random value used to stagger or vary spawned helper/timer behavior for visual timing.
Spawn a short-lived helper/timer process used as a delay between animation/effect steps.
Start/execute an inferred lightning visual/effect process alongside the temporary item visual.
Create a temporary item/entity used to represent the lightning/visual effect and store its reference in a local.
Position the created temporary item near the NPC (using Z/Y/X with offsets) to place the visual effect in the world.
Play a lightning/hit sound effect during the visual sequence.
Advance the temporary item's animation frame as part of the visual effect sequence.
Spawn an additional helper/timer used as a short pacing delay before cleanup/damage application.
Destroy the temporary visual item after the animation completes to clean up the effect.
Apply the actual hit/damage to this NPC (passes magnitude/speed, direction from Npc::getDir, and context), representing the gameplay consequence of the hit.
Obtain the NPC's facing/direction to pass into receiveHitWrapper for correct hit direction application.
Yield the process between spawned helper timers and animation frames to time the sequence.

reads.name: alreadyHitLightningZapper
reads.likely_effect: Gate whether the lightning visual/hit sequence may run (prevents immediate retriggers).

writes.name: alreadyHitLightningZapper
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 at sequence start to lock out further triggers and cleared back to 0 at sequence end to re-enable triggering.

When the process receives a hit with a nonzero attacker/item parameter, it checks a guard flag (alreadyHitLightningZapper) and whether the incoming item overlaps this NPC. If conditions pass, it sets the guard to prevent re-triggering, spawns short-lived helper timers, creates and positions a temporary visual item, plays a lightning SFX, animates that temporary item across several frames with timed suspends, destroys the visual item, calls receiveHitWrapper to apply the actual hit/damage to the NPC (using the NPC's facing direction), then clears the guard flag so future hits may trigger again.
